question: You have production and test workloads. Production VMs need to be in a different subnet than test VMs, but both must reach each other over Internal IP without additional routes. Which configuration meets this?

options.A: Create a single custom VPC with 2 subnets. Create each subnet in a different region and with a different CIDR range.
options.B: Create a single custom VPC with 2 subnets. Create each subnet in the same region and with the same CIDR range.
options.C: Create 2 custom VPCs, each with a single subnet.
options.D: Create 2 custom VPCs with the same CIDR.

explanation: Internal communication is default within the same VPC even across regions, provided CIDR ranges don't overlap[cite: 1].

question: IPs for nodes are exhausted in a VPC-native GKE cluster. How to ensure clusters can grow?

options.A: Create a new subnet in the same region.
options.B: Add an alias IP range to the subnet.
options.C: Create a new VPC and peer it.
options.D: Expand the CIDR range of the relevant subnet.

explanation: Expanding the primary CIDR range of a subnet is the standard way to provide more IPs for nodes[cite: 1].

question: Multi-player gaming app needs consistent performance for global users with minimal complexity.

options.B: Use Cloud Spanner to store user data.
options.D: Bigtable partitioned by username.

explanation: Cloud Spanner offers global scale and strong consistency with low management overhead[cite: 1].

question: VMs with only internal IPs need to access a Cloud Storage bucket without internet access.

options.A: Enable Private Service Access on the Bucket.
options.B: Add storage.googleapis.com to VPC Service Controls.
options.C: Enable Private Google Access on the subnet.
options.D: Deploy a Cloud NAT instance.

explanation: Private Google Access allows internal-IP-only VMs to reach Google services[cite: 1].

question: Need to move a startup project into your organization and update billing with minimal effort.

options.A: Use the projects.move method and update the billing account.
options.B: Use Resource Manager to drag and drop.
options.D: Use Terraform to redeploy and delete old.

explanation: The projects.move API is the most efficient way to transfer projects between organizations[cite: 1].

question: External member needs 'list access' to images and disks. What to grant?

options.A: Create a custom role with compute.disks.list and compute.images.list and grant it at project level.
options.B: Grant Compute Image User role with extra permissions.
options.C: Grant Compute Storage Admin and exclude permissions.
options.D: Grant Compute Storage Admin directly.

explanation: Creating a custom role with only the required permissions follows the 'Least Privilege' principle[cite: 1].

question: App has critical parts and fault-tolerant parts. How to optimize for cost?

options.B: Single pool Spot VMs.
options.C: Critical on Spot, fault-tolerant on Standard.
options.D: Critical on Standard node pool, fault-tolerant on Spot node pool.

explanation: Critical tasks need reliability (Standard), while fault-tolerant tasks save money on Spot VMs[cite: 1].

question: Enable communication between frontend (vpc-a) and backend (vpc-b) in different projects cost-effectively.

options.B: Create VPC Network Peering.
options.D: Cloud Interconnect.

explanation: VPC Peering is the most efficient and cost-effective way to connect VPCs internally[cite: 1].

question: Configure policies/log sinks that project users cannot remove, different for each department.

options.B: Configure policies on projects directly.
options.C: Organize projects under folders for each department and configure policies on folders.
options.D: Configure on organization level only.

explanation: Folder-level configuration allows for department-specific policies that project owners cannot override[cite: 1].

question: API quota errors in Cloud SQL logs during traffic spikes on Cloud Run. How to mitigate?

options.A: Modify the minimum number of Cloud Run instances.
options.B: Use traffic splitting.
options.C: Modify the maximum number of instances.
options.D: Set minimum concurrent requests.

explanation: Increasing minimum instances keeps connections 'warm', reducing the need for repeated API calls for new instances[cite: 1].

question: Stateless app must be reachable from internal IP in VPC and on-premises with minimal infrastructure management.

options.B: Cloud Run with Private Google Access.
options.C: Cloud Run with Private Service Connect.
options.D: GKE Autopilot with Private Google Access.

explanation: Private Service Connect allows Cloud Run services to be reached via internal IP addresses[cite: 1].

question: VM needs a fixed IP for other services in the same VPC to communicate with it, minimizing cost.

options.B: Promote the existing IP to a static internal IP.
options.D: Reserve new static external IP.

explanation: Static internal IPs are cost-effective and allow reliable communication within a VPC[cite: 1].

question: Test service account permissions from a local machine securely.

options.B: Grant SA BigQuery Administrator role.
options.C: Configure gcloud CLI to use service account impersonation.
options.D: Use Application Default Credentials with user account.

explanation: Impersonation is more secure than using service account keys[cite: 1].

question: Ensure only company-issued accounts access GCP and users stay within their department resources.

options.B: Use Google Groups for IAM and organization policies to block non-company emails.
options.C: Grant Folder Admin to users on their department folder.
options.D: Grant Folder Admin to all users at organization level.

explanation: Organization policies provide strict enforcement against unauthorized domains[cite: 1].

question: Cloud Run service needs to reach GKE API privately.

options.A: Expose GKE via internet and use Cloud Armor.
options.B: Create ingress firewall rule for 0.0.0.0/0.
options.D: Internal ALB on GKE, Cloud DNS, and Serverless VPC Access connector on Cloud Run.

explanation: Serverless VPC Access allows serverless products to reach internal VPC resources like an Internal ALB[cite: 1].

question: Be notified of any modifications to Cloud Storage files with minimal overhead.

options.B: Enable versioning and configure Pub/Sub notifications.
options.D: Object change notifications to Pub/Sub.

explanation: Pub/Sub notifications for Cloud Storage provide an automated way to track object changes[cite: 1].

question: Deploy microservices that cannot be exposed publicly with minimal management overhead.

options.B: Install Kubernetes on Compute Engine.
options.D: Standard zonal GKE cluster.

explanation: Autopilot manages nodes for you, reducing management overhead while keeping services private[cite: 1].

question: Pods need to authenticate to Cloud Storage API without service account keys.

options.A: Grant node service account bucket access.
options.B: Use Workload Identity Federation to link Kubernetes SA with Google SA.
options.D: Security exception for 8-hour keys.

explanation: Workload Identity is the most secure and recommended way for GKE pods to access Google Cloud services[cite: 1].

question: Lift-and-shift migration for apps with hard-coded internal IPs.

options.B: Create non-overlapping VPC and assign new static IPs.
options.C: Create VPC with same CIDR and assign same static internal IPs.
options.D: Single subnet with Cloud NAT.

explanation: Matching the CIDR and IPs avoids the need to change application code during a quick migration[cite: 1].

question: Manage Linux account lifecycle and access for developers.

options.A: Enable OS Login for all VMs and use IAM roles.
options.B: Custom startup scripts for SSH keys.
options.D: OS Login with custom startup scripts.

explanation: OS Login simplifies SSH management by linking it to IAM identities[cite: 1].

question: Operations team needs specific permissions on GKE and Cloud SQL using two predefined roles.

options.A: Combine permissions into one custom role.
options.B: Grant two predefined roles directly.
options.C: Create custom role with ONLY required permissions.
options.D: Grant full Admin roles for both services.

explanation: Custom roles tailored to specific needs follow the principle of least privilege[cite: 1].

question: Automate provisioning across GCP and two other cloud providers.

options.B: Use Terraform.
options.D: YAML manifests.

explanation: Terraform is a multi-cloud standard for infrastructure as code[cite: 1].

question: Need granular breakdown of Google Cloud costs in BigQuery.

options.A: Enable Cloud Billing data export to BigQuery.
options.B: Link billing account and view table in BigQuery.
options.D: Enable BigQuery API and select data location.

explanation: Exporting billing data to BigQuery is the standard way to perform detailed cost analysis[cite: 1].

question: Static website and asynchronous HTTP background job. Need zero cost when not in use.

options.A: GKE for site, Cloud Functions for job.
options.B: Compute Engine for both.
options.D: GKE for site, Compute Engine for job.

explanation: Cloud Run can host both and scales to zero, meaning no cost when idle[cite: 1].

question: Allow all production VMs to communicate via private IP and accept connections on specific ports via organization-level rules.

options.A: Individual VPCs with peering and organization policies.
options.B: Individual VPCs with peering and hierarchical firewall policies.
options.C: Host VPC with service projects and organization policies.
options.D: Host VPC with service projects and hierarchical firewall policies.

explanation: Shared VPC (Host/Service projects) and hierarchical firewall policies provide centralized networking and security management[cite: 1].

question: Deploy new version with no startup time (warm instances).

options.A: Don't serve immediately; use simulation tool.
options.B: Set service minimum instances to 2.
options.D: Set existing revision minimum instances to 2.

explanation: Minimum instances on the specific revision ensure instances are ready before traffic arrives[cite: 1].

question: Stateful app needs more CPU during peak times.

options.B: Vertical Pod Autoscaler.
options.D: Cluster autoscaling.

explanation: VPA is used for scaling pod resources vertically for stateful applications[cite: 1].

question: Visualize cost data in multiple ways on the same dashboard.

options.B: Cloud Billing export to BigQuery and Looker Studio.
options.D: Monitoring metrics to BigQuery and Looker.

explanation: Looker Studio combined with BigQuery billing data allows for complex, multi-view dashboards[cite: 1].

question: Simplify SSH access management while maintaining audit compliance.

options.B: Metadata SSH keys.
options.C: Enable OS Login via organization policy.
options.D: OS Login with 2FA.

explanation: Enforcing OS Login via organization policy centralizes SSH access management[cite: 1].

question: Fetch and acknowledge waiting messages from a subscription.

options.B: pubsub topics describe.
options.D: pubsub topics list-subscriptions.

explanation: The 'pull' command with '--auto-ack' fetches and confirms receipt of messages[cite: 1].
